programme.title: Confessions of a Cam Girl
programme.desc: The naked truth on sex camming with the intimate stories of different girls building empires from their bedrooms - like Maya, who quit her medical degree to go on camera full-time.

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment
programme.desc: Natalie and Tom are left questioning everything after some steamy intimacy with Josh and Holly. Chanice and Richardo invite 15 of the residents for an orgy, but being in a room with so many people having sex becomes overwhelming for Richardo.

programme.title: 24 Hours in AandE
programme.desc: At Queens Medical Centre in Nottingham, staff help a 16-year-old with a serious illness. Steve is rushed into Resus after falling from his motorbike. And 63-year-old Paul has come to AandE after partially amputating his thumb at work.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Location, Location, Location
programme.desc: Kirstie and Phil catch up with two couples who wanted to buy close to the English Riviera in Torquay. Newlyweds Sam and Jamie wanted to put a difficult couple of years behind them, while Geoff and Sharon were searching for a large family home.

programme.title: Stacey Solomons Renovation Rescue
programme.desc: Stacey helps couple Katie and Chris, whove lost their renovation confidence after a bad experience with tradespeople. She encourages them to face their demons and get stuck in, showing them how to tackle some ambitious jobs.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: On a spectacular second night in Bedfordshire and Cambridgeshire, self-styled culinary explorer Dave serves up a daring menu melding flavours that no one else would ever think of combining - there might be a reason for that!

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Twins Sara and Paula host the weeks second visit at their boutique hotel in Devon, The Horn of Plenty. After an afternoon on the lash at a local vineyard, are there sour grapes in the feedback?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: Seasonal deadlines crank up the heat for Aggi and Camille in France and Angie and Ash in Italy. In Portugal, summer strain also bites for Harriet and Mondos holiday cottages.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Angie and Alan want to fulfil their dream of buying their very first property together on Spains beautiful Orihuela Costa. Can Jasmine Harman find the perfect place for their £60k?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: On Malta, we see Chris new kitchen from Italy no less! On the Costa Blanca, we revisit Jan and Bill at Casa Milly, that they named in honour of Bills mum.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In Lincolnshire, the first to host is university lecturer Amanda Sherratt, who is taking a risk with a full Japanese-themed evening, including a raw fish starter and sumo wrestling for entertainment.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the turn of retail manager Ali Roberts in Lincolnshire. Ali is confident that her night of gourmet cuisine and elegant fine dining will win her the prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Farmer Chris Moore plans to win over his guests with his beetroot-themed dinner party in Lincolnshire, managing to inject beetroot into every part of the night and surprising his guests with his unusual twists on conventional meals.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its day four in Lincolnshire, and Jack Stone plans to win over his guests with his `creative evening, the theme includes getting the guests to make their own food.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The week of competitive dining in Lincolnshire concludes with proud Geordie Alex Coxon taking a turn as host. Alexs menu includes dishes called `wey-aye-man!, `a taste of toon and `a canny little dessert like.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Danni Menzies helps Poppy and her dad Philip realise their dream of finding a holiday home in Andalucía for their budget of £50k, that all the family can use to enjoy the Spanish sunshine.

programme.title: George Clarkes Amazing Spaces
programme.desc: A VW van gets a transformation with a twist, a brick bomb shelter is converted into a bijou bar, and George and Will forge ahead with their ultimate indoor-outdoor living project.

programme.title: Inside the Worlds Most Luxurious...

programme.title: Batch from Scratch: Cooking for Less

programme.title: The Great Celebrity Bake Off: Stand Up To Cancer
programme.desc: Comedians Joe Wilkinson, Roisin Conaty, Rose Matafeo, Judi Love, Tom Davis and Jon Richardson take on breakfast pastries, matcha and yuzu biscuits, and the weirdest places theyve ever slept, in cake form!

programme.title: Jon Richardson: Old Man
programme.desc: Jon Richardson returns to the stand-up circuit to complain about the world and offer no solutions, on topics including parenthood, Brexit, haircuts, and the question of why nobody but him can correctly load a dishwasher.

programme.title: Sean Lock: Keep It Light
programme.desc: Sean Lock presents his blend of surreal yet insightful stand-up comedy, recorded at the Theatre Royal in Margate, in which he airs his mischievous views on parenting, old age, and immigration.

programme.title: Open House: The Great Sex Experiment

programme.title: Tanked
programme.desc: From rare sea creatures and exotic fish to feisty crabs, Wayde and Brett share some great stories about the coolest and craziest fish theyve ever built tanks for.

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: EAMT Marisa assists an injured stray dog that they suspect has been hit by a car. Meanwhile, Guy deals with a goat with a broken leg.

programme.title: Animal Cops Phoenix
programme.desc: EAMT Amy Jo investigates a complaint of dogs being neglected in south-west Phoenix. When she arrives, she finds five dogs in ill health, but the owner refuses to cooperate.

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: Wes and Robert remove eggs from the nest of dangerous saltwater crocs Terra and Bosco. Meanwhile, Bindi tends to the training of otters Percival and Daisy.

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: After great flood of 2016 devastates Louisiana, Tia and the Villalobos team help the victims. An elderly woman is hospitalised and separated from her dogs.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: George and Beth are prisoners in their own home due to their vicious kitty Reese. Reese is a cat version of Jack the Ripper and Beth is one of his choice targets.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: Barnabas didnt turn out to be the cuddler that Alan and Shannon wanted, but rather a hissing, scratching, biting demon cat whose only kryptonite was opera music.

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Following Texas game wardens as they protect the vast wildlife and natural resources of Texas.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: While Dr Amy tackles exploratory surgery on a cat with a strange appetite, Dr Jeff helps an injured tiger.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.desc: Pete descends upon western Washington to make a tree house for action and relaxation. With a 55-foot-long bouncy-bridge entryway into a 20-foot-high structure and a zip line that travels through the forest, this tree house generates non-stop thrills.

programme.title: The Pool Master
programme.desc: Anthony helps a busy firefighting farmer and his wife fix a project that started as a muddy hole and turn it into a natural pool complete with an outdoor rock shower and fire hydrant.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: At the head of the bay, Atz Sr. divulges the secrets of the range rider to Atz Lee and Elvin and Otto, Charlotte and August must transform more than 1,000 pounds of metal pipe into a working chimney.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.sub-title: Bam Bams Big Build and Bears
programme.desc: A hungry predator encroaches, forcing Bird and Gabe to take stronger action. Bam Bam must play nice with his siblings to break ground on his homestead.

programme.sub-title: Lady in White / Thump in the Night
programme.desc: Animals begin reacting to something no one can see. Soon, the humans began seeing what the animals were seeing: the ghost of a woman who once lived near the house.

programme.desc: Wes and Robert remove eggs from the nest of dangerous saltwater crocs Terra and Bosco. Meanwhile, Bindi tends to the training of otters Percival and Daisy.

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: The family make their annual trip to the Steve Irwin Wildlife Reserve to study wild crocodiles, and continue the mission started by Steve.

programme.title: Pit Bulls and Parolees
programme.desc: Tia attempts to rescue a stray, but her leg injury holds her back, and new parolee Mike struggles to balance working and being a parent.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: Marcos cat Monty eats anything in sight from shoes to bedspreads, yet Marco blames his new bride Aimee for enabling Montys bad habit, Leilani and Michael had a happy two-cat household until Leilani brought in two more.

programme.title: My Cat From Hell
programme.desc: Frankie the cat pees all over Daniel and Desirees new multi-million dollar dream home. Plus, cat Sugar is hiding from newly adopted cat Dyna.

programme.title: Lone Star Law
programme.desc: Following Texas game wardens as they protect the vast wildlife and natural resources of Texas.

programme.title: Dr. Jeff: Rocky Mountain Vet
programme.desc: Dr Jeff operates on a dog rushed to the clinic after a growth on her neck ruptures. Meanwhile, the team performs a risky procedure on a pair of rescued miniature donkeys.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.sub-title: A Treehouse Fit for a Viking
programme.desc: Pete takes on his first international build in Norway. Working with local builder, Frode, the 24 ft high retreat gives stunning views of a nearby lake.

programme.title: Rugged Justice
programme.desc: Father and son arrested on separate charges; fisherman has illegal firearm; orphan bear released.

programme.title: Alaska: The Last Frontier
programme.desc: Otto, Eivin and August rush to stake a claim on priceless steel, Atz Sr, Bonnie, Atz Lee and Jane try to stockpile salmon for the winterand Shane and Kelli break ground on their cabin.

programme.title: Alaskan Bush People
programme.desc: When an ostrich lays its first egg, the Brown siblings rush to work out how to incubate it. Also, Bear gets a special visit from a girl on North Star Ranch.

programme.title: The Haunted
programme.desc: A family and their dogs are being frightened by the spirit of someone who may have once lived in their home. Fearing for their lives, they seek help from a paranormal team.

programme.title: Crikey! Its the Irwins
programme.desc: The family make their annual trip to the Steve Irwin Wildlife Reserve to study wild crocodiles, and continue the mission started by Steve.

programme.title: The Pool Master
programme.desc: Anthony helps a busy firefighting farmer and his wife fix a project that started as a muddy hole and turn it into a natural pool complete with an outdoor rock shower and fire hydrant.

programme.title: Treehouse Masters
programme.sub-title: A Treehouse Fit for a Viking
programme.desc: Pete takes on his first international build in Norway. Working with local builder, Frode, the 24 ft high retreat gives stunning views of a nearby lake.

programme.title: Animal Cops Philadelphia
programme.desc: When an emaciated dog is brought into the Pennsylvania SPCA shelter weighing a third of its normal body weight, the team find the dogs owner also needs their help.

programme.title: Asmaan Se Ooncha
programme.desc: Kishan saves Anita from a thug, Naag Raj. Later, they fall in love and lead a happy life. However, their happiness is short-lived as Naag Raj returns to their lives to seek revenge.

programme.title: The Real Hunter
programme.desc: A village politicians son comes back to his village after his graduation. He intends to settle down in his village, but his father wants him to get married to a girl from a very affluent family, to help his political ambitions.

programme.title: Jaan Se Pyaara
programme.desc: A cop looks after his mentally challenged twin brother and devoted mother. However, his dangerous police work gets in the way of family life, causing tragedy.

programme.title: Maati Maangey Khoon
programme.desc: As far as he can remember Harinarain Singh alias Hariya has no knowledge of his birth, parents nor of a family home. During a skirmish with some of Thakur Balram Singhs hoodlums, he lies unconscious at the entrance of a temple with the Poojary in attendance, and an elderly unhinged woman praying to God for his sake.

programme.title: Mera Faisla
programme.desc: A young man wishes to marry the love of his life, Rati Verma, but her father wants nothing to do with him because of a stigma attached to their family name.

programme.title: Karishma Kudrat Ka
programme.desc: A 1985 action film in starring Dharmendra and Mithun Chakraborty in lead roles supported by Rati Agnihotri, Anita Raj and Shakti Kapoor.

programme.title: Barah Aana
programme.desc: Three strangers stumble into a crime and discover its not a bad business.

programme.title: Chalte Chalte
programme.desc: A young couple are in love and to be married, only for the groom to tragically pass away. For the bride it is all too much, and she finds herself in a mental hospital. Years later, upon her release, she discovers that the man she loved did not die after all.

programme.desc: Mitch and Caroline rescue teenage lovers who attempt to commit suicide; Cody and C.J. encounter what they believe to be a mermaid.

programme.desc: Cody realizes he has been taking his relationship with C.J. for granted when a secret admirer comes to her rescue after an accident.

programme.title: A History of Britain by Simon Schama
programme.desc: The story of Edward I - known to many as the villainous king in the Hollywood film `Braveheart - who tried to force English rule on Scotland and Wales, but found the resultant slaughter only served to inspire bold declarations of independence. When the king was eventually forced to listen to the parliament of his own people, England would also learn what it meant to be a nation.

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: A Storyville documentary, set against the backdrop of conflict in Sudan, that follows five Khartoum residents as they search for freedom amid revolution and war.

programme.title: The Girl Who Talked to Dolphins
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the most extraordinary experiment in the history of animal science. In the 1960s a powerful and charismatic scientist flooded a house. He then invited a young woman to live there full-time with a dolphin.

programme.title: The Unseen Alistair Cooke
programme.desc: A revealing portrait of one of the most celebrated broadcasters of the 20th century, Alisdair Cooke, whose Radio 4 programme `Letter from America spanned 58 years. Seen for the first time are extraordinary 8mm home movies shot by Cooke from 1933 onwards, charting his discovery of America, his passions and his friendships, including his close friendship with Charlie Chaplin.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Jacqui is not impressed by the mushrooms growing inside a Norfolk end-of-terrace. In the West Midlands, Dion thinks some walls need to be rebuilt. Martel loves the story behind how a foster father and his son paired up as property developers.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles heads to Detling in Kent, where experts Catherine Southon and Mark Stacey assist the teams in their search for profitable antiques to take to auction. Eric also visits Maidstone Museum to see a rare collection of ceramics that offer an insight into Japanese philosophy.

programme.title: Just One Thing
programme.desc: In Sheffield, empty nesters Siva and Dhami are feeling stuck in a rut. But Roman Kemp is about to shake things up. He wants to show them how taking up dancing could do far more than just put a spring in their step. Research reveals that adding dance to your weekly routine can boost fitness, increase brain power and lift your mood. A little rhythm might just rewrite Siva and Dhamis whole routine.

programme.title: The Travelling Auctioneers

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and his crew turn cowboy, heading for the marshlands of Argentina. Theres a nasty surprise in store for Steve as he searches for an anaconda. Undeterred, he then swims in piranha-infested waters.

programme.title: Boarders
programme.desc: The boarders try to impress when they meet the parents - but will they succeed? Abby is confronted by Tobys mum about his future, and Cheddars dad gives Femi a daring challenge.

programme.title: Serengeti
programme.desc: All around is a blackened smoking wasteland, the aftermath of the huge fire. Torrential rain brings hope and the land is reborn into a green paradise as water quenches the scorched earth. Mirroring the changes in the landscape, the cycle of death and rebirth comes to the fore as the Serengetis elders prepare their young for adulthood and a life on their own.

programme.desc: Bing and Sula are performing a puppet show for Flop, Amma and Pando when Coco and Charlie arrive. Coco wants to join in with her Rainbow Fairy Mice. programme.desc: Duggee is a big, friendly dog who runs The Squirrel Club, an after-school activity club where children can learn and take part in all kinds of activities, have fun adventures, and earn badges for their achievements.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Muffin is playing Grouchy Granny at a garage sale, making Dad push her on an old mobility scooter. When a real Grouchy Granny wants to buy it, Muffin wont budge.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Dad eats her last chip, Bingo is given three chances to put Mum and Dad in `Dance Mode as a form of payback. The game involves flicking someones tail when theres music on to send them into an endless dance.

Flop arrives, bringing his sleeping bag and Hoppity so they can all sleep over.

programme.desc: Mrs Weaver raps the alphabet with Duggee and the Squirrels.

programme.title: Lets Go for a Walk
programme.desc: Ranger Hamza and the Ramblers walk through the town to the place where they can do their recycling. On the way, they climb a lookout tower, listen to the wind blowing in the trees, play musical statues and even beep like reversing buses!

programme.title: Our Family
programme.desc: Audrey has a table tennis lesson with her coaches Tim and Harry. First, she practices some bat and ball skills before enjoying a rally against Harry, who is an actual table tennis champion. Later, the whole family get sporty and have some fun playing mini-golf, and Audrey puts Mum through her paces with a very bendy yoga lesson.

programme.title: My World Kitchen
programme.desc: Disas family comes from the Scandinavian country, Sweden. She is baking kardemummabullar, which is a Swedish recipe for cardamom buns. Disa makes a milk mixture, sweetened with honey, and mixes it into flour to make a dough.

programme.title: Teeny Tiny Creatures
programme.desc: Elena finds out all about marvellous moths. Chantelle is with Dr Amir Khan to see one of the worlds biggest moths while Rory and his son Sandy are in disguise being moth spies.

programme.title: Dog Squad
programme.desc: Meagaidh the Border Collie is a search and rescue dog who works with her owner Tom. Her supernose follows scents and smells like clues, so they can rescue people who need their help. Joe and his daughter Kate are in trouble at the top of a really steep hill. They are lost, cold, soggy, tired and hungry. And theyve dropped their picnic bag down a hill. Joe manages to contact Tom at the Woofton Search and Rescue Centre, just before the battery on his phone runs out.

programme.title: Go Wild
programme.sub-title: Aneeshwar and Lizzie: European Bison and Red Billed Hornbill
programme.desc: Aneeshwar and Lizzie meet bison and red-billed hornbills.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: Waffles paw is hurt in class, so Vet Jess trains Mr Nolan in doggy first aid. Doug invents a device with buttons that Waffle can press for entertainment if he gets bored resting.

programme.title: Puffin Rock
programme.desc: Oona and Silky meet some scary creatures and help Bernie to get home safely.

programme.title: Small Potatoes
programme.desc: Meet potatoes from around the world as they celebrate the similarities and differences we all share, with a zydeco tune.

programme.title: Yukee
programme.desc: Everyone is bringing presents to the opening of Twinkles zen garden, and Yukee wants to take something too. But when she cant find anything, they encourage her to sing about her frustration.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee arranges for the Squirrels to write to some pen pals, helping them to learn all about life in far away places, earning their Pen Pal badges and making some new friends in the process.

programme.title: Yakka Dee
programme.desc: Yakka Dee! celebrates children communicating - because talking fills you up with whizz, pop, bang! In this episode, Dee encourages a friend to say car and we go on a whirlwind word adventure. We see lots of different beds - a big bed, a baby bed, even a bed for a bear! There are bunk beds, twin beds, comfy beds and jumpy beds. Dee dresses as a princess and climbs into a lumpy bed, then falls asleep on a moon bed.

programme.title: Numberblocks
programme.desc: Three has lost one of her buttons! Can detective Numberblock One help find it?

programme.title: Alphablocks
programme.desc: Plusman to the rescue! A new superhero arrives in Alphaland to save the day by joining words together.

programme.title: Something Special
programme.desc: Justin and a friend make a space rocket and play mini golf. Meanwhile, Lord Tumble attempts to teach Mr Tumble how to play golf, but things dont quite go to plan.

programme.title: Teletubbies
programme.desc: Tinky Winky cant sleep and goes outside to play so as not to wake the other Teletubbies. But Tinky Winkys noisy footsteps, on top of Home Dome, wakes them up!

programme.title: Patchwork Pals
programme.desc: The Patchwork Pals have a problem: Snake is coming by, but her visit is way too short. Will she find a way to make everybody happy?

programme.title: Raa Raa the Noisy Lion
programme.sub-title: Raa Raa Plays Do What I Do
programme.desc: Raa Raa is playing a new game called Do What I Do with his friends, but a misunderstanding upsets Topsy.

programme.title: Chuggington
programme.sub-title: Chug Encounters of the Train Kind
programme.desc: Adventure ensues when the trainees think they are being visited by Chuggers from outer space.

programme.title: Gigglebiz
programme.desc: DIY enthusiast DIY Dan builds a birdbath for all the little garden birds. Arthur Sleep keeps getting distracted whilst trying to give a very important piece of news. Sticky toffee pudding is on the menu in Dina Ladys Bake Me a Star competition and there are some very hungry slugs in Will Barrows garden.

programme.title: Andys Prehistoric Adventures
programme.desc: Andy travels 100 million years back into prehistoric times to meet spinosaurus, a massive meat-eating dinosaur even bigger than a T-Rex. On his adventure he rides a speedy ouranosaurus and takes a dip in a prehistoric river.

programme.title: The Game Catchers
programme.desc: The Game Catchers are five friends who travel through space exploring fantastic planets with one big mission: to discover, play and collect all the games in the universe.

programme.title: Octonauts
programme.desc: A sea sponge that houses other sea creatures in its pores suffers from a mysterious illness and Peso must figure out the cause.

programme.title: Peter Rabbit
programme.sub-title: The Tale of the Lost Ladybird
programme.desc: When Peter accidentally loses Lilys ladybird, Florence, the rabbits embark on a dangerous chase to recover her precious pet.

programme.title: Hey Duggee
programme.desc: Duggee takes the Squirrels and their parents on an exciting mystery trip to a surprising destination, where they learn they can make anywhere special and earn their Big Day Out badges in the process.

programme.title: Vida the Vet
programme.desc: Vida and Popcorns search for a mysterious stink in the koala family home leads to young Pippen receiving a much-needed lesson in flossing.

programme.title: Supertato
programme.desc: Supertato invites Evil Pea to stay with him at Tato Tower, but she soon takes it over.

programme.title: Weasy Family
programme.desc: The Ducklings throw a no adults party, so Weasy parties solo but makes a huge mess.

programme.title: Mojo Swoptops
programme.desc: Capn Gails tall tale of a nine-legged octopus in Lake Swoppiton has everyone panicking. But Mojo and Bo wonder: is the story real or is Capn Gail up to something?

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: Muffin is playing Grouchy Granny at a garage sale, making Dad push her on an old mobility scooter. When a real Grouchy Granny wants to buy it, Muffin wont budge.

programme.title: Piripenguins
programme.desc: After a lab mishap, Loophs new size-o-matic machine misfires, causing her to shrink! Now tiny, can she find a clever way to alert her friends and turn everything back to normal.

programme.title: Bluey
programme.desc: When Dad and Uncle Stripe play a game of squash, Bluey and Bingo pretend that they are in control of their every move. Bingo proves that big siblings dont always beat little siblings.

programme.title: Andys Dino Island
programme.desc: When Jens drone goes missing on Dino Island, Andy heads out on an epic adventure to track it down. He discovers one of the islands inhabitants, a giraffe-sized pterosaur called Hatzegopteryx, is using the drone for a very special purpose. Along the way, he meets a herd of stampeding triceratops.

programme.title: Waffle the Wonder Dog
programme.desc: The Brooklyn-Bells take Waffle to school to ask if he can visit class. When Headteacher, Mr Nolan, says no, Waffle goes missing, hes determined to prove hes ready for school.

programme.title: Rafi the Wishing Wizard
programme.desc: Rafi, Ruby and Finn play football against Khias team. Rafi is goalie but keeps missing shots and feels under pressure. She makes a wish for giant hands to save every ball. When the wish ends, Rafi worries shell lose, but Ruby and Finn remind her shes already a great goalie. Believing in herself, Rafi makes a fingertip save on Khias penalty and wins the game fairly.

programme.title: JoJo and Gran Gran
programme.sub-title: Its Time to Catch the Bus
programme.desc: JoJo and Gran Gran miss their bus home, so the pair settle down to wait for the next one. But getting the bus isnt quite as simple as JoJo thinks it is. A wrong bus later and the pair are back where they started!

programme.title: Sarah and Duck
programme.desc: Cake decides to teach Sarah and Duck how to decorate with icing.

programme.title: Clangers
programme.desc: Mother Wol asks the Iron Chicken if she will look after her three little Wols while she goes to get some things for her new nest. The Iron Chicken is happy to do so, but she hasnt counted on them being so boisterous.

programme.title: Beddybyes
programme.desc: MeMo and BaBa explore up, down and upside-down in many different forms.

programme.title: In the Night Garden
programme.sub-title: Where Is the Pinky Ponk Going?
programme.desc: Everyone in the Night Garden boards the Pinky Ponk for a very special journey. Up, up, up they go. Where can the Pinky Ponk be going?

programme.sub-title: Gaz Coombes - Octopants: The Missing Pirate Pants
programme.desc: A different story read each night just before bedtime.

programme.desc: In New Orleans, Gordon Ramsay visits family-run restaurant The Verdict, where the owners evil mother and chef frequently loses control of the kitchen - and her temper.

programme.title: Channel 4 News Summary
programme.desc: Channel 4s midday news summary.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole, Simon OBrien and Max McMurdo travel across the UK to find unique items lost in peoples homes to fix up for a profit. This episode features a Raleigh shopper bike.

programme.title: Matt Baker: Our Farm in the Dales
programme.desc: Lambing season continues, and the family build a field shelter to keep an eye on the ewes. And Matt and Mike add some finishing touches to the stable yard.

programme.title: Countdown
programme.desc: Colin Murray, Rachel Riley and Susie Dent host, with Jon Kay in Dictionary Corner, as contestants race against the clock to pit their wits against vowels, consonants and numbers.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Claire and Simon are starting a new adventure in Nerja in Spain. Will any of Craig Rowes options for their £230k get their hearts racing enough to put in an offer?

programme.title: A New Life in the Sun
programme.desc: In Bulgaria, dad and son Colin and Paul turn a barn into an entertainment space. In Spain, Jo and Ken tackle a lull in business, and Becky and Kay prep for their first guests.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ussy and Bobsey host at Optimal Apartments, their luxury self-catering digs in the heart of Bradford. Unfortunately, some of their guests arent sure that flat-pack furniture equates to luxury.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Peppe hosts an authentic Italian night, but one of his guests doesnt like Italian food! Will his Neapolitan nosh go down well, or will some hamster high jinks hamper his chances?

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: What Happened Next
programme.desc: On Cyprus, is Uri and Hazels vivacious villa still delivering the dream? And we see what Karmen has done to her meant-to-be Mijas Pueblo bolthole.

programme.title: Channel 4 News
programme.desc: Includes sport and weather.

programme.title: The Dog House
programme.desc: Corina and Honey the cavapoo get off to a shaky start. But Honey has a trick or two that might just turn things around. And is Ruby the perfect teammate for football-mad Josh?

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Eight contestants remain - and one decides its time to abandon their role as underdog. As fortunes flip in the forest, a dramatic twist turns the game on its head, putting even the strongest players in peril ahead of a tense and unexpected climax.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Gogglebox
programme.desc: Britains sharpest armchair critics share more of their sharp, insightful, passionate and sometimes emotional critiques of the weeks biggest and best shows on television.

programme.title: Britains Most Expensive Houses
programme.desc: The brokers at UK Sothebys International Realty are back to secure more multimillion-pound sales. Lee has an incredible grade I listed terraced home to sell overlooking Regents Park lake. And Guy has been invited to sell a rather unusual houseboat.

programme.title: Ramsays Hotel Hell
programme.desc: Gordon continues to restore the failing Towns Inn in Harpers Ferry, West Virginia. After witnessing abysmal food hygiene practices in the restaurant, Gordon is left in disbelief as he uncovers terrifying cleanliness issues throughout the hotel.

programme.title: The Hunt: Prey vs Predator
programme.desc: Deep in a vast, remote forest, 10 players enter an extraordinary contest of stamina and strategy, all competing to win up to £100,000. The players are pitted against each other as Prey versus Predator, and will battle to survive action-packed hunts.

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Offering their unmatched satirical insight into current world events, Adam, Josh and Alex are joined by Jack Dee, GK Barry and Jason Williamson from Sleaford Mods to help dissect the biggest news stories of the week.

programme.title: The Last Leg
programme.desc: Offering their unmatched satirical insight into current world events, Adam, Josh and Alex are joined by Jack Dee, GK Barry and Jason Williamson from Sleaford Mods to help dissect the biggest news stories of the week.

programme.title: Insomnia! The All Night Party!
programme.desc: Fancy a mash up after midnight? Only massive, ad free anthems to help with your insomnia! More beats, less sleep!

programme.title: A Decade of Beats! The 90s
programme.desc: Need a fix of 90s club classics? Lock in for a marathon of memories as we take you back to greatest decade of dance! The 90s!

programme.title: Clublands Million Sellers! Top 30
programme.desc: Thirty of the biggest beats of all time, from the 1990s to 2018 - and theyve all sold a million! No single act appears twice in our chart, so expect a few surprises.

programme.title: Take Me To The Clouds Above!
programme.desc: The sound that started it all! Join us for a selection of legendary Clubland classics. Expect high-energy beats, the biggest vocal anthems and feel good party vibe for all!

programme.title: Forgotten Clubland Classics!
programme.desc: Weve opened the Clubland vaults and we are delving deep. Prepare for some less played deep dives and forgotten gems from archives!

programme.title: Utah Saints: Warehouse Daze Top 30
programme.desc: 30 tracks. One legendary era. Join Utah Saints and experience the early 90s warehouse rave scene with their ultimate setlist. This is for the Old Skool massive!

programme.title: The Old Skool Originals!
programme.desc: Lets go way back with some classic throwback hits, long forgotten beats and some good old fashioned rave anthems!

programme.title: The Proper Clubland 50!

programme.title: Take Me To The Clouds Above!

programme.title: Every Number Beat of the 00s

programme.title: 100% Pure Rave Classics!
programme.desc: Going in a little harder and moving away from the tunes that dominated the charts of the early 90s.

programme.desc: A neglected wife develops feelings for a coworker in one of four stories.

programme.desc: It is about Rajiv and Payal who are friends since childhood. While Payal is in love with Rajiv, it takes him a bit longer to return her feelings.

programme.desc: Family drama following the lives of a group of college students.

programme.desc: Naqaab brings together director duo Abbas-Mastan with another thriller. The focus of the film shifts to the shady past of all the characters, with some unexpected elements thrown in.

programme.desc: In this sequel to the Bollywood hit movie, Bajirao Singham returns as the brave and honest policeman who settles every dispute in his town with his words of wisdom, and resorts to force only when necessary.

programme.title: Dum Laga Ke Haisha
programme.desc: A marriage is arranged between a slim guy and an over-weight girl.

programme.desc: When a cruel landowner, Jaswant Singh, assaults an old woman, her son, Jeeva, steps in to intervene, and is forced to leave the village. Years later he returns to execute revenge on Jaswant.

programme.title: Friends
programme.desc: Ross learns that a pregnant Rachel wants to move out and find a place of her own, so he suggests a vacancy in his building. programme.title: Accident, Suicide, or Murder
programme.desc: A wealthy widows sudden death perplexes investigators. But with conflicting causes of death and millions at stake, suspicion falls on those closest to her.

programme.title: Spring Break Murders

programme.title: Interrogation Raw

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Deal or No Deal
programme.desc: Airing from 2005 to 2009 on NBC, the game show series returns to primetime TV with original host Howie Mandel. A contestant must choose from 26 sealed briefcases containing a marker for various amounts of cash -- from one penny to $1 million. The player then eliminates the remaining 25 cases one by one. The chosen ones are opened and the amount of money inside revealed. After several cases are opened, the player is tempted by the Banker to accept an offer of cash in exchange for not continuing the game and possibly winning a larger sum of money.

programme.title: Gold Rush
programme.desc: The boys are back in Alaska (although Discovery dropped the states mention in the title after the first season) hoping to strike it rich mining for gold. Their first effort was anything but rich and glorious, however, as the crew led by Todd Hoffman and his father, Jack, sunk $250,000 into their dream and came away with just $20,000 worth of gold. The dismal summer was filled with injuries, malfunctioning equipment and constant fighting among the greenhorn miners, yet their serious case of gold fever trumps any talk of giving up and leads to a new operating plan in order to salvage their dream. The series also keeps tabs on the efforts of teenager Parker Schnabel and old-timer Dakota Fred to hit the mother lode at separate sites.

programme.title: The Transporter 2
programme.desc: Professional driver Frank Martin is living in Miami, where he is temporarily filling in for a friend as the chauffeur for a government narcotics control policy-maker and his family. The young boy in the family is targeted for kidnapping, and Frank immediately becomes involved in protecting the child and exposing the kidnappers.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: All That Heaven Allows
programme.desc: A widow falls in love with her much-younger gardener, but town gossip and the wrath of her children mar their romance.

programme.title: Heaven Knows, Mr Allison
programme.desc: A Roman Catholic nun and a hard-bitten US Marine are stranded together on a Japanese-occupied island in the South Pacific during World War II. Under constant threat of discovery by a ruthless enemy, they hide in a cave and forage for food together. Their forced companionship and the struggle for survival forge a powerful emotional bond between them.

programme.title: The True Story of Jesse James
programme.desc: Borrowing widely from Nunally Johnsons script for Henry Kings 1939 version of the legend (and using two sequences from the same production), Nicholas Rays version portrays Jesse James as a rebel rather than a simple outlaw, driven to a life of crime by Northern sympathisers after the Civil War as the film follows the last 18 years of his life.

programme.title: The Ghost and Mrs Muir
programme.desc: Anna, a recent widow, moves out of her in-laws home to a haunted English cottage where she falls in love with the ghost of a 19th-century sea captain that once lived there.

programme.title: Gemini Man
programme.desc: Henry Brogan is an elite assassin who becomes the target of a mysterious operative who can seemingly predict his every move. To his horror, he soon learns that the man whos trying to kill him is a younger, faster, cloned version of himself.

programme.title: Theater Camp
programme.desc: Eccentric staff members of an upstate New York theatre camp must band together when their beloved founder falls into a coma.

programme.title: xXx
programme.desc: An extreme sports junkie is blackmailed by the CIA into taking on a highly dangerous mission to Prague in order to thwart the plans of a group of Russian extremists. The reluctant hero soon finds himself with the safety of the USA in his hands - and a complicated romance on the horizon.

programme.title: Beautiful Beings
programme.desc: Addi, a gifted boy raised by a clairvoyant mother, decides to adopt a bullied misfit into his gang of outsiders. Left to their own devices, the boys discover aggression, violence, loyalty, and love.

programme.desc: Featuring free-range geese in Leicestershire, bitter from Southwold, some delicious kippers, and free-range pork from the Suffolk Black.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Heroes
programme.desc: In Lancashire, Rick samples a traditional hotpot and some mouthwatering cheese, and in the Lake District he finds wild boar and the famous Herdwick sheep.

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers Go North
programme.desc: The Hairy Bikers are exploring an area close to Daves heart - Cumbria and the Lake District. The Lakes is the most visited national park in England, and with such incredible scenery, its not hard to see why.

programme.title: The Pioneer Woman
programme.desc: Ree Drummond tackles a busy morning of ranching and get-ahead cooking. She creates the ultimate meatball and polenta casserole and hasselback chicken.

programme.desc: Jamie Oliver cooks up his snappy sesame seared tuna, a delicious meal in minutes, and messy meatball buns, feel-good family food thats fast and loaded with flavour, followed by his irresistibly gooey chocolate rye cookies.

programme.desc: Inas got vegetable ideas for every day of the week, when youre stuck in a vegetable rut. There are fantastic main courses, starters, salads and sides.

programme.desc: The baking challenge is back, welcoming the tents youngest-ever baker and the oldest. Theyll be cooking a Swiss roll, Marys classic cherry cake, and 36 miniature British cakes.

programme.desc: The bakers cook biscuits for a cheese course, Marys Florentines and finally a 3D biscuit scene showstopper.

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food

programme.title: Jamies Comfort Food
programme.desc: Jamie takes on three comfort food classics. First up is a retro treat: chicken Kiev. He also cooks spaghetti vongole with a non-traditional pink twist, and creates a deluxe shepherds pie - made with slow-roasted lamb shoulder instead of mince.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In New Yorks Lower East Side, pickling, smoking, curing and fermenting, everything from the goat necks to chicken wings, in Eureka, Calif., Mediterranean favourites, in Miami, Fla., whole fried snapper and beer braised baby back ribs.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: Eateries in Key West, FL, include a little shack serving up New England seafood, standout fish tacos, a tiny food cart serving Asian-Caribbean fusion dishes.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Miami, Fla., loaded chimi burrito and lobster lollipops; in Sandy, Utah, Canadian bacon and potato croquettes and white bean and steak pizza; in Salt Lake City, ten ounce meatballs.

programme.title: Diners, Drive-Ins and Dives
programme.desc: In Miami, Fla., comfort food like spicy oxtail and big ragu; in Park City, Utah, chivito club and chicken bowl with rice; a mom and pop ski lodge serving up pork osso buco, vegetarian mushroom stroganoff and crispy pork belly.

programme.desc: The pursuit of Americas greatest chow-downs has led Adam Richman to Omaha, Nebraska. Adam begins his culinary journey by trying the famous Godfather sandwich at Amatos Cafe.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs Michelin starred Frances Atkins, determined Paul Welburn and returning champion Colin McGurran go head to head with their fish courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The North East chefs Frances Atkins, Paul Welburn and Colin McGurran attempt to impress the veteran judge with their main courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the North East chefs last chance to impress with their desserts as only two will get to cook for the judges. Frances Atkins serves another dish inspired by her fathers involvement in D Day.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining chefs for the North East fight it out for one place in the finals, cooking all four of their courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis must do everything they can to impress the surprise veteran judge wholl be scoring their dishes with their starter courses and theyre each drawing on personal inspiration.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Mary Ellen McTague, Jason Athertons protege James Durrant and fellow newcomer Mark Ellis go head to head for the fish course.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its main course day, and after the disastrous fish course Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis are all desperate to impress the veteran judge.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its Mary Ellen McTague, James Durrant and Mark Ellis last chance to impress with their desserts as at the end of todays cooking one chef must leave the competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining North West chefs cook their four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort, who must decide which chef will win a place in the finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of London and the South Easts superstar chefs, returning contender Adam Simmonds, Michelin-starred newcomer Tom Sellers and his former boss Adam Byatt, attempt to dazzle the mystery judge with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: London and South East chefs, Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt continue their battle. Does a trip to Adam Byatts nans house to find out about his grandfathers military connections spur him on to fish course glory?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The London and South East chefs Adam Simmonds, Tom Sellers and Adam Byatt go head to head cooking their main courses. At the crucial half way stage the chefs must do all they can to score highly.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert course time as the London and South East chefs compete for one of two places in the judges chamber and the competition couldnt be tighter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a tightly fought battle today as the two remaining London and South East chefs cook all four of their dishes again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge, Joy Hunter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the Central regions top chefs, returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett go into battle. They begin with their starter courses.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Returning champion Aktar Islam, Daniel Cliffords protege Mark Poynton and ambitious newcomer Jason Hodnett fight it out for fish course glory. After plating up late last time will Aktar manage to be on time today?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The Central region chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poynton and Jason Hodnett go head to head cooking their main courses for the veteran judge. With Mark and Aktar fighting for the lead can Jason sneak under the radar to take them by surprise?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its dessert day and the Central chefs Aktar Islam, Mark Poyton and Jason Hodnett are all striving for one of two spots in the regional final. Will Jasons elaborate chocolate medal dessert see him triumph or sent home empty handed?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Central region chefs cook all four courses again for the judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by special guest judge Max Hastings.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the starter course and as the chefs arrive they discover theres added competition following the London and South East regions shock result. The judges are joined by D-Day veteran George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the fish course and those chefs who didnt make it onto the shortlist with their starters are hoping to do better. The judges are joined by Ken Sturdy who landed on Sword Beach on D-Day and who helped judge the North West region.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the half-way point in finals week and those chefs who have yet to get a dish onto the shortlist are feeling the heat. The judges are joined by Baroness Trumpington who worked as a cypher clerk at Bletchley Park from 1941 to 1946.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the last chance for the chefs to get their dish in contention for the final banquet and those whove failed to finish in the top three so far are going all out. Joining the judges is Celia Sandys, Winston Churchills granddaughter.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the moment of truth as the victorious four must put their finest hours on a plate for those who served both on the front line and the home front, such as D-Day veterans Ken Sturdy and George Batts.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of Scotlands top chefs, Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee, and Graham Campbell - attempt to impress this weeks veteran judge. Having missed out last year, returning finalist Jak is determined to make it to the banquet.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell fight it out for the fish course. Graham is in a confident mood but returning finalist Jak and self-taught Jimmy are pushing him hard. Will the pressure prove too much for ambitious Graham?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Scottish chefs Jak ODonnell, Jimmy Lee and Graham Campbell are battling to plate up a perfect main course for this weeks veteran chef. Self-taught Jimmy is honouring his mum with his rendition of her Chinese beef stew.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Today its the Scottish chefs last chance to impress, with the dessert course. Only two can cook for the judges tomorrow, and with one chef in the lead after a triumphant main, the pressures on for the other two to plate up perfection.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining Scottish chefs cook their entire menus again for the judges - Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Theyre joined by WI Calendar Girl Angela Baker, who will help decide which chef will go forward to the National Finals.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Three of the south wests finest chefs - Jude Kereama, Josh Eggleton and Dominic Chapman - attempt to impress a veteran judge with their starters. As the only newcomer in the kitchen, Jude is determined to plate up a winning dish.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The three south west chefs fight it out for the fish course. Despite schoolboy errors during the starter, experienced Dominic is determined to get a high score on the course. But will newcomer Jude, a seafood master, overcome his nerves to plate up?.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course as the three south-west chefs go head to head. Newcomer Jude is brimming with determination and confidence as the main course battle hits the kitchen.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The south-west chefs have one last chance to impress as they face the dessert course. Only two chefs can go through to cook for the judges; with everything to play for, who will plate up perfection and who will be sent packing?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: The two remaining south-west judges must cook their entire menus again for judges Prue Leith, Oliver Peyton and Matthew Fort. Joining the judges is Radhika Bynon, a member of the Forest Gate Womens Institute, a modern urban group of women.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael are challenged to plate up perfect starter courses for this weeks veteran judge. Its the first time in the Great British Menu kitchen for all three chefs, so who will triumph and who will fall?

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Stephen Gomes, Adam Bannister and Phil Carmichael fight it out for the fish course. Experimental chef Stephen is determined to prove himself with a fish course honouring the WIs tradition of preserving, but hes up against stiff competition.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its a battle for the main course with the Welsh chefs going head to head. With all three chefs paying homage to Welsh lamb; Adam with a refined faggot, Phil with a dish dedicated to his Mum and Stephen with a lamb dish celebrating the WI.

programme.title: Great British Menu
programme.desc: Its the tricky dessert course and the last chance for the Welsh chefs to secure their place in tomorrows regional final. With everything to play for, the pressure is fierce. Only two can make it through, and one will leave the competition for good.

programme.title: Hitlers Secret Weapons Manager
programme.desc: Did the man behind Hitlers secret weapons programme survive the war? Was SS General Hans Kammler covertly brought into the USA to safeguard his knowledge? New evidence on a vexing enigma.

programme.title: Secrets Of Hitlers Island Fortress
programme.desc: Guernsey, the only British isle occupied by Nazi Germany during WWII, reveals unique wartime history.

programme.title: Hitlers Jurassic Monsters
programme.desc: Dramatic reconstructions show how Goerings ethnic cleansing went hand in hand with his attempts to actually recreate extinct Germanic beasts like the Aurochs.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: What persuaded one-time "Man of the Year" Pierre Laval to send Jews to the death camps?

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Uncovers the motives behind the people and organisations who made the choice to aid the Nazi war effort.

programme.title: Nazi Collaborators
programme.desc: Viktor Arajs formed a Latvian commando unit that hunted Jews.

programme.title: The Mistake that Killed Hitler
programme.desc: In April 1945, as Stalins Red Army approach from the east and the western Allied forces quickly approach through France and Belgium, Adolf Hitler awaits his fate in his bunker, reflecting on the mistakes that lost him the war.

programme.title: Great American Generals - Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, Bradley
programme.desc: A compilation of archive footage provides insight into what made Patton, MacArthur, Eisenhower, and Bradley heroes of American history.

programme.title: Nazi Fugitives
programme.desc: The Nazi regime lasted from 1933 to 1945 and was one of the most horrific periods of history. However, Adolf Hitler was not working alone. He had a circle of some of the most barbaric men alongside him, who helped make the atrocities possible.

programme.title: Dan Snow And Frank Mcdonough Talk Adolf Hitler
programme.desc: Dan Snow and Frank McDonough discuss Hitlers health and medication with a Third Reich expert.

programme.title: Stalins French Fighters
programme.desc: 1942: France offered Normandy-Niemen squadron to Stalin, claiming victory, 43 pilots killed.

programme.title: Catherine De Medici The Black Queen Of France
William the Conqueror
programme.desc: In 10066, William the Conqueror is about to embark from Dives-sur-Mer for the conquest of England. In the event that he does not come back alive, Guillaume presents his son Robert to his faithful barons.

programme.title: Independence Or Death: The Haitian Revolution
programme.desc: Haitian Revolution exposed slaverys brutality and overturned Frances plantation colony.

programme.title: 1979: Women of the Iranian Revolution
programme.desc: The 1979 Iranian Revolution, led by Khomeini, imposed the veil and sparked a fight for freedom.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher discovers what life was really like for ordinary Egyptians 3,500 years ago, during the height of the incredible civilisation of ancient Egypt. Joann explores how people lived during this time, from the tightly packed houses and villages of the common people, to the clothes they wore and the food they ate.

programme.title: Ancient Egypt: Life and Death in the Valley of the Kings
programme.desc: Egyptologist Dr Joann Fletcher reveals a strange and mysterious world: the ancient Egyptian afterlife. To them waking life was just a dress rehearsal. Joann clambers into rarely visited tombs, explores a treasure trove of long-buried objects and examines spectacular mummies to discover just why the Egyptians spent a fortune preparing for death.

programme.title: Egypt: Behind the Revolution
programme.desc: Filmed primarily in Egypt in March of 2011, soon after the January revolution, this film introduces you to the Egyptians who lived under Mubarak and helped to bring him down.

programme.title: Cuba: The Forgotten Revolution
programme.desc: Cuban revolutionaries Frank País and Jose Antonio Echeverría play critical roles in the overthrow of Fulgencio Batista.

programme.title: The Russian Revolution
programme.desc: The Russian Revolution in 1917 changed the course of history. Two families go to war: Lenins Ulyanovs and the Tsars Romanovs. With assassinations, popular revolt, and a daring seizure of power, the twentieth century is changed forever.

programme.title: Simon Sebag Montefiore on Stalin
programme.desc: Find out how Stalin climbed to the top of Soviet politics and hear how his paranoia shaped the way he ruled.

programme.title: Dan Snow And Frank Mcdonough Talk Adolf Hitler
programme.desc: Dan Snow and Frank McDonough discuss Hitlers health and medication with a Third Reich expert.

programme.title: Stalins French Fighters

Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Alice Roberts follows the excavation of Iron Age Britains most spectacular grave. A team of archaeologists in East Yorkshire have uncovered the remains of only the third upright chariot burial ever found in Britain.

programme.title: Ross Kemp: Mafia and Britain
programme.desc: Ross Kemp investigates and exposes Britains surprising and deep-rooted links with the mafia, dating as far back as the 1920s.

programme.title: Greatest Prison Escapes With Billy Hayes
programme.desc: After scaling walls and eluding officers to escape from three separate high security prisons in the 50s, Alfie Hinds earned the nickname Houdini.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the turbulent history of Carrickfergus, one of the most ancient castles in Northern Ireland and a stronghold that holds the key to understanding the tempestuous relationship between Britain and Ireland.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Edinburghs handsome hilltop bastion is Scotlands most popular paid tourist attraction. With a slew of sieges to its name, it is no surprise that the site boasts some formidable defences, and Dan puts one heavy duty cannon through its paces.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores Manchesters history of protest and revolution during the Industrial Revolution, from Marx to the Suffragettes and the Peterloo massacre.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts tells the story of Roman Britain by studying the history of Chester, Britains most Roman town.

programme.title: Britains Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: A host of top celebrities explore uniquely British preoccupations and passions in an attempt to find out what makes the British tick.

programme.title: Britains Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: Suggs discovers the history of the British love of the humble hostelry and explores a load of interesting issues along the way, from womens role in brewing, and the community role of the pub, to the pub as a place of literary and musical creativity.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores 2019s most exciting archaeological finds.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the North, the remains of a Tudor house in Leicestershire were thought to be the childhood home of Englands forgotten Queen, Lady Jane Grey. But when archaeologists excavate, they find more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Edinburghs handsome hilltop bastion is Scotlands most popular paid tourist attraction. With a slew of sieges to its name, it is no surprise that the site boasts some formidable defences, and Dan puts one heavy duty cannon through its paces.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones explores the history of Cardiff castle over more than 2,000 years and reveals how it became transformed from a strategically important Roman and Norman stronghold to a lavish fairy-tale Victorian home.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts explores the English Civil War through the history of Oxford, the city at the heart of a battle between an autocratic king and his rebellious parliament.

programme.title: Britains Most Historic Towns
programme.desc: Alice visits Canterbury for the story of Plantagenet England. Canterbury is steeped in history and the era of the Plantagenets - the strong house that held the English throne from 1154 until 1485 - saw the most dramatic episodes in the citys story.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: The dramatic moors and valleys of West Yorkshire are the setting for an unusual slice of the past in Tony Robinsons first walk from this new series. This evocative landscape was the home of, and inspiration for, the Brontes, the remarkable literary family that produced Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits the Cairngorms National Park to discover how Queen Victoria and Prince Albert helped to shape Scottish traditions and imagery such as tartan, bagpipes and caber tossing.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts travels back to the Ages of Bronze and Iron to discover what kind of a place Britain was before the Romans invaded. With no written history, only archaeology can provide the clues. Alice uncovers a world that is complex, sophisticated and pretty strange. She examines the two Hebridean Bronze Age skeletons known as the Cladh Hallan Mummies.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Dr Alice Roberts goes in search of our elusive Stone Age ancestors. Along the way she visits the Channel island of Jersey where she meets a team of archaeologists hoping to shed new light on the much-maligned Neanderthals, and embarks on a kayak survey of the coastline looking for undiscovered sites hidden in the cliffs. At the Natural history museum Alice sees the dark side of our Ice Age ancestors lives.

programme.title: Britains Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: Chris Packham sets out to discover when animals first became our "best friends", and which kind of pets Brits prefer. During his investigation he meets a pet psychic and a real-life pet detective, he then visits a rescue centre for exotic pets.

programme.title: Britains Greatest Obsessions
programme.desc: Lorraine Kelly explains our constant harking back to Winston Churchill and the part played by Britain in the Allied victory.

programme.title: Cracking the Code
programme.desc: Revealing the greatest codes known to humanity and the brilliant minds that manage to solve them.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson visits Sherwood Forest and the Peak District as he sets out to discover the real King John.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A listed building in Brentwood in Essex dating back to the 16th-century is sold under the hammer, along with a flat in Halesowen in Birmingham and an end terrace in Northwood in Stoke-on-Trent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large semi in New Barnet in London is bought as an ambitious project to turn into a family home, a family do up a semi in Dover in Kent to make a profit, and two friends club together to try their hand at refurbishing a terraced house in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large Victorian semi packed full of original features in Dresden in Stoke-on-Trent, a two-bed semi in Croydon in London and some public loos in Kincardine in Fife are sold at auction. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners and hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A huge 1930s house in Kidderminster in Worcestershire, a purpose-built flat in a 1980s block in Wembley in North London, and a substantial Victorian semi in Burslem in Staffordshire are sold at auction.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A semi in Folkestone in Kent, a terrace in Hanley in Stoke-on-Trent and a huge detached house in Seascale in Cumbria are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners to hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house with period features in Forest Gate in London, a house with a stream but close to a railway line in Handsacre in Staffordshire, and a large three-storey property in Plymouth are all sold at auction.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A third-floor flat in Walworth in London, a piece of land in Derbyshires Chesterfield, and a block of flats in Hengoed in Mid Glamorgan are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell visits a house in Guildford, Martin Roberts falls in love with a Grade II-listed house in Torquay in Devon, and Dion Dublin is in Barry in Wales assessing all the work needed on a mid-terrace house. Will the developers plans run smoothly?

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel talks to a 20-year-old developer whose 4th property is in Madeley Heath, Dion meets two friends refurbishing a flat in Birkenhead, and Martin talks to identical twin brothers who have ambitious plans for a derelict fire station in Paisley.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A bungalow in Callington in Cornwall, a three-bed semi in Willenhall near Wolverhampton and a terraced house in Hertfordshires Watford are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Dion and Martel meet the new owners, and hear their plans for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy Alexander talks to experienced developers who deliver a stunning renovation on a subsidence-troubled semi in Coulsdon in Surrey, Dion Dublin meets two brothers with ambitious plans for a commercial property in Derby, and Martel Maxwell meets with two golfing friends turned developers who plan to turn round a one-bedroom flat in Telford in Shropshire in record time.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bed detached house ripe for extending in Bilston near Wolverhampton, a cosy cottage in Nettlestead Green in Kent and a terraced house in Etruria in Stoke-on-Trent without a bathroom or an inside loo are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A garage with development potential in Margate in Kent, a ground-floor flat in a Victorian house in Shepherds Bush, London, and a piece of land ripe for building on in Birmingham are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a one-bedroom house in Canterbury, Kent, a three-bedroom semi in Chaddesden, Derby and a pub in Rotherham, South Yorkshire. All of these properties have been sold at auction and well find out who bought them and what they paid.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin and Lucy visit a flat in Chiswick, London, a five-bedroom house in Derby and a spacious mid-terraced house in Blythe, Northumberland. All of these properties have been sold at auction and well find out who bought them and what they paid.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Emmerdale is one of Britains best-loved and highly-viewed long-running drama soaps - set in the heart of the beautiful countryside of the Yorkshire Dales, this realistic and down-to-earth drama superbly chronicles the joys and the heartbreaks of a tightly-knit countryside community.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The disappearance of a young boy at Denton Football Club leads Frost into a disturbing story of child abuse and paedophilia. In addition to the usual pressures in his life, Frost must also decide whether or not to accept a promotion to Chief Inspector.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Jeff Randall is confined to his bed. He has met with an accident - a fall from a balcony - and hes therefore a captive audience when Marty Hopkirk insists on telling of a case he handled entirely on his own, without mentioning it to Jeff, before he met his death and became a ghost. Jeff is forced to listen in disbelief to a remarkable spy drama which began with a telephone call asking for him.

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: PC Nick Rowan investigates the strange case of the house-proud housebreaker, and Aidensfield has seen a spat of burglaries recently and in each one only silverware has been stolen. However, a common and baffling clue in every case is the fact that the thief tidies the house. Rowan also becomes suspicious of the do-gooder, considered by many to be a `saint.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When a prize-winning author is murdered on the eve of a literary festival, Barnaby and Scott discover a hotbed of corruption, scandal and financial mismanagement in the community of Midsomer St Michael. Before they can discover who is telling the truth, the death toll begins to rise.

programme.title: This is LegendXtra
programme.desc: Home to movies and series full of horror, sci-fi, fantasy, thriller and much more.

programme.title: The Lost World
programme.desc: Challenger, Malone and Veronica are exploring ruins on a riverbank, when a man stumbles out of the jungle in a hail of deadly arrows. Malone drags the wounded man to safety, who dies in their arms issuing a cryptic message to find a man named Pierson Rice. Challenger recognises the name as that of a vain glorious hunter whom Roxton once held in high regard.

programme.title: The Fall Guy
programme.desc: A drug dealer requires the services of a little person to retrieve his well-hidden stash.

programme.title: Triple 9

programme.title: Mercenaries
programme.desc: A former British serviceman leads a special ops team on a mission to rescue a kidnapped ambassador.

programme.title: The Resident

programme.title: King Solomons Mines

programme.title: Best of the Best
programme.desc: Martial arts drama in which a misfit team of American kick-boxers must undergo rigorous training for an all-important contest against a ruthless Korean team. Along the way, the hardened fighters learn to lay their personal ghosts to rest.

programme.title: Buried
programme.desc: US contractor Paul is working in Iraq when one day, after an attack by a group of Iraqis, he wakes up to find he has been buried alive inside a coffin, somewhere in the desert. With just a cigarette lighter and a mobile phone on him, he must somehow find a way to escape before the air supply runs out.

programme.desc: The planet Mercury is struck by a solar flare that sends it out of orbit and on a collision course with Earth. programme.title: Four in a Bed

The accommodation proves a winner with Mark and Fee and John and Neil, but Mrs Tee is proving more difficult to please.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon OBrien revamp and repurpose hidden treasures. On the Isle of Wight, Henry finds a three-wheeled motorbike. Simon turns a projector screen into a work of art.

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Lorraine and Jon want to fulfil their long-held dream of owning a house in the whitewashed mountain villages of Axarquía in Spain. Jean Johansson shows them what their £110,000 can buy.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: This weeks competition comes from the West Midlands where first host, company director Sue, promises an evening her guests will never forget.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: In the West Midlands, business guru Marc intends to use his coaching skills to plan a night that will impress his guests and bag the prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the third day in the West Midlands and 48-year-old teaching assistant Kim is hoping that her summer holiday themed evening means that she will be swanning off into the sunset with the £1000 prize.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the fourth day in the West Midlands, and 27-year-old college manager and part-time comedian Paul is turning his home into a `Black Country boozer as he attempts to pull in the points as well as pulling pints.

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Its the last night in the West Midlands, and mole-catcher Michael intends to impress his guests with his night of fancy French cuisine.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The competition kicks off at Brook Meadow, near Market Harborough in Leicestershire. Interior designer host Claire is banking on her expertise and picturesque location to be the winning combination, and her guests are certainly dazzled by the decor.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit of the week is to The Apollo Inn, in Barlborough, Derbyshire. Host James hopes that his hospitality skills will win the week, and the rooms above his pub make a good first impression, until the bedding rubs Claire up the wrong way.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The BandBers are in Letton in Herefordshire for the third visit of the week, at The Swan Inn, run by Luke and Heather. Their freshly renovated coaching inn has expectations high, and the guests are impressed by how sparklingly new everything is.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final hosts this week are Steph and Martin, who welcome the BandBers to White Mark Glamping in Watlington, Oxfordshire. Some of their guests arent quick to embrace the great outdoors, especially upon discovering the communal shower blocks.

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Its payment day, and time for some score settling. One host comes under fire for the harsh criticisms they left throughout the week, and things get emotional before the winner is revealed.

programme.title: Kirstie and Phils Love It or List It

programme.title: Peppa Pig
programme.desc: George has fun making bubbles, and Peppa has fun popping them.

programme.desc: Daddy teaches Peppa and George a fun game to play while they wait inside for the rain to stop.

programme.desc: In the early hours of June 6, 1944 - D Day - the invasion of France begins. A million and a half men take part. It is the bloodiest day in American history since the Civil War.
